addedBlocks:
## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Next: theurge-owns-suite-composition (₢BJAAE) [rough]

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Architecture move requiring judgment — one load-bearing risk (single-process fixture isolation) gates an otherwise mechanical collapse.
  
  ## Goal
  
  Make theurge the sole owner of suite→fixture composition. Suite names survive only as tabtarget imprints; no suite name or fixture list appears anywhere in bash.
  
  ## Locked constraints
  
  - Supersedes the "suite is bash composition" decision (₢BBAAh, ₣BB) — a release-qual conformance call, not a principled ownership stance.
  - Suite membership is expressed in Rust by reference to the existing `RBTDRC_FIXTURES` statics, so a bad member fails at compile time — not a name-string list mirrored across the boundary.
  - The dirty-tree guard and per-run root allocation move to once-per-suite; today they run once per fixture.
  
  ## Shape
  
  - Rust gains a suite registry and the binary a suite mode (suite name in, its fixtures run); the single-fixture and `single` modes stay. Bash's suite arrays, the suite-resolve, and the per-fixture loop collapse to an imprint pass-through; the suite-rationale comments (gauntlet state-walk, skirmish/dogfight operator preconditions) migrate to Rust doc on the registry.
  - Load-bearing risk: the per-fixture loop becomes one process instead of N. Confirm crucible quench runs on the case-failure path, and settle quench-on-panic, before relying on the in-process loop.
  
  ## Sources
  
  - `Tools/rbk/rbtd/rbte_engine.sh` — suite arrays, resolve, `rbte_suite` loop
  - `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/main.rs` — binary modes, dirty-tree guard, root allocation
  - `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs` — `RBTDRC_FIXTURES` fixture statics
  - the `rbw-ts.TestSuite.*` suite tabtargets — imprint channel
  
  ## What done looks like
  
  Suite composition lives only in theurge; grep finds no suite names or fixture lists in bash; suite tabtargets pass only their imprint; a typo'd suite member fails compilation; all suites run green.

content.content:
1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BJ
2	
3	## Paddock: rbk-14-mvp-testing-simplification
4	
5	## Shape
6	
7	Theurge — the crucible test orchestrator — should be a clean *consumer* of
8	operator tabtargets, never an owner of commands and never a reacher into module
9	internals. This heat closes the two places where it still violates that.
10	
11	**1. Access-probe relocation.** Theurge currently owns the `rbtd-ap` colophon
12	family (the credential access probes), the only colophon it owns. This work
13	retires that family into four operator credential-check tabtargets
14	(`rbw-acg/acr/acd/acp`) dispatched by `rbw_workbench`, leaving theurge owning
15	zero colophons. The `rbgv_AccessProbe.sh` implementation is unchanged — only
16	ownership and dispatch move. Mechanical; the docket carries the full tabtarget
17	mapping and done-when.
18	
19	**2. Contract-surface migration.** Theurge's fast-suite validation cases
20	bypass tabtargets — hardcoding module source paths, replicating kindle chains,
21	calling `z`-private functions. This work moves them onto contract surfaces.
22	
23	## Locked decisions / findings (from grooming survey, 260520)
24	
25	- **Sequencing behind ₣BK.** Both work items sit inside ₣BK's moorings-cutover
26	  blast radius (the same `rbtd-ap` tabtargets, `rbtdrp_pristine.rs`, and
27	  `rbtdrf_fast.rs` that ₣BK's filesystem-rename and literal-sweep touch). This
28	  heat mounts only *after* ₣BK lands. Doing it after — not concurrently —
29	  removes all concurrent-edit risk; and both work items shrink ₣BK's surface
30	  (the access-probe work deletes four tabtargets; the migration work strips the
31	  hardcoded `.rbk/` path literals that ₣BK's literal-sweep names as its
32	  canonical example). Operator holds this schedule.
33	
34	- **The trampoline form is settled.** ₣BK's tabtarget trampoline already landed.
35	  New operator tabtargets are the one-line `exec .../z-launcher.sh rbml_rbw
36	  "${0##*/}" "${@}"` form — they carry no `.buk`/`.rbk` literal and are immune
37	  to ₣BK's later launcher-path flip.
38	
39	- **The migration is one file.** `rbtdrf_fast.rs` is the sole bypass-heavy
40	  surface; the sibling theurge test files already route through tabtargets. The
41	  `rbtdrf_run_tt` / `rbtdrf_run_tt_neg` helpers already exist. regime-smoke and
42	  dockerfile-hygiene families already use tabtargets.
43	
44	- **The work splits by family, not by pace.** regime-validation is the real
45	  migration target (candidate surfaces `rbw-rrv/rvv/rnv`). enrollment-validation
46	  is pure-utility (BUV type checks via public `buv_vet`) — leave it, do not
47	  invent a test-only tabtarget for it. Kept as a single study-gated pace;
48	  decompose only if the crux below forces invented test-API.
49	
50	- **Pivotal unknown (settle first at mount).** Whether a validate tabtarget can
51	  be pointed at a *staged malformed regime* decides whether regime-validation
52	  negatives are tabtarget-coverable (mechanical) or need a declared
53	  `*_t_validate` entry point (a design sub-task).
54	
55	- **Reliability spine.** A pre-migration verdict baseline (per-case Pass/Fail
56	  across the fast suite) is the safety net — the post-migration verdict set must
57	  match exactly, and migrated negatives must fail for the *same reason*, not
58	  merely fail.
59	
60	## Done looks like
61	
62	Theurge owns no colophons; the four credential checks are operator tabtargets.
63	`rbtdrf_fast.rs`'s regime-validation family exercises tabtargets (or declared
64	test entry points), with no hardcoded module paths or `z`-private reach. Fast,
65	complete, and gauntlet suites pass with verdicts matching the pre-migration
66	baseline.
67	
68	## References
69	
70	- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrf_fast.rs` — migration surface
71	- `Tools/rbk/rbgv_AccessProbe.sh` — access-probe implementation (unchanged)
72	- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/rbte_cli.sh`, `rbte_engine.sh` — theurge colophon ownership to strip
73	- `Tools/rbk/rbz_zipper.sh`, `rbw_workbench.sh` — operator dispatch enrollment
74	- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrm_manifest.rs` — fixture-to-colophon dependency tables
75	- `rbtdri_invocation.rs` header — "theurge invokes bottle operations exclusively through tabtargets"
76	
77	# jjezs_pace ₢BJAAE
78	
79	## Character
80	
81	Architecture move requiring judgment — one load-bearing risk (single-process fixture isolation) gates an otherwise mechanical collapse.
82	
83	## Goal
84	
85	Make theurge the sole owner of suite→fixture composition. Suite names survive only as tabtarget imprints; no suite name or fixture list appears anywhere in bash.
86	
87	## Locked constraints
88	
89	- Supersedes the "suite is bash composition" decision (₢BBAAh, ₣BB) — a release-qual conformance call, not a principled ownership stance.
90	- Suite membership is expressed in Rust by reference to the existing `RBTDRC_FIXTURES` statics, so a bad member fails at compile time — not a name-string list mirrored across the boundary.
91	- The dirty-tree guard and per-run root allocation move to once-per-suite; today they run once per fixture.
92	
93	## Shape
94	
95	- Rust gains a suite registry and the binary a suite mode (suite name in, its fixtures run); the single-fixture and `single` modes stay. Bash's suite arrays, the suite-resolve, and the per-fixture loop collapse to an imprint pass-through; the suite-rationale comments (gauntlet state-walk, skirmish/dogfight operator preconditions) migrate to Rust doc on the registry.
96	- Load-bearing risk: the per-fixture loop becomes one process instead of N. Confirm crucible quench runs on the case-failure path, and settle quench-on-panic, before relying on the in-process loop.
97	
98	## Sources
99	
100	- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/rbte_engine.sh` — suite arrays, resolve, `rbte_suite` loop
101	- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/main.rs` — binary modes, dirty-tree guard, root allocation
102	- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs` — `RBTDRC_FIXTURES` fixture statics
103	- the `rbw-ts.TestSuite.*` suite tabtargets — imprint channel
104	
105	## What done looks like
106	
107	Suite composition lives only in theurge; grep finds no suite names or fixture lists in bash; suite tabtargets pass only their imprint; a typo'd suite member fails compilation; all suites run green.
108

# jjezs_paddock ₣BJ

## Paddock: rbk-14-mvp-testing-simplification

## Shape

Theurge — the crucible test orchestrator — should be a clean *consumer* of
operator tabtargets, never an owner of commands and never a reacher into module
internals. This heat closes the two places where it still violates that.

**1. Access-probe relocation.** Theurge currently owns the `rbtd-ap` colophon
family (the credential access probes), the only colophon it owns. This work
retires that family into four operator credential-check tabtargets
(`rbw-acg/acr/acd/acp`) dispatched by `rbw_workbench`, leaving theurge owning
zero colophons. The `rbgv_AccessProbe.sh` implementation is unchanged — only
ownership and dispatch move. Mechanical; the docket carries the full tabtarget
mapping and done-when.

**2. Contract-surface migration.** Theurge's fast-suite validation cases
bypass tabtargets — hardcoding module source paths, replicating kindle chains,
calling `z`-private functions. This work moves them onto contract surfaces.

## Locked decisions / findings (from grooming survey, 260520)

- **Sequencing behind ₣BK.** Both work items sit inside ₣BK's moorings-cutover
  blast radius (the same `rbtd-ap` tabtargets, `rbtdrp_pristine.rs`, and
  `rbtdrf_fast.rs` that ₣BK's filesystem-rename and literal-sweep touch). This
  heat mounts only *after* ₣BK lands. Doing it after — not concurrently —
  removes all concurrent-edit risk; and both work items shrink ₣BK's surface
  (the access-probe work deletes four tabtargets; the migration work strips the
  hardcoded `.rbk/` path literals that ₣BK's literal-sweep names as its
  canonical example). Operator holds this schedule.

- **The trampoline form is settled.** ₣BK's tabtarget trampoline already landed.
  New operator tabtargets are the one-line `exec .../z-launcher.sh rbml_rbw
  "${0##*/}" "${@}"` form — they carry no `.buk`/`.rbk` literal and are immune
  to ₣BK's later launcher-path flip.

- **The migration is one file.** `rbtdrf_fast.rs` is the sole bypass-heavy
  surface; the sibling theurge test files already route through tabtargets. The
  `rbtdrf_run_tt` / `rbtdrf_run_tt_neg` helpers already exist. regime-smoke and
  dockerfile-hygiene families already use tabtargets.

- **The work splits by family, not by pace.** regime-validation is the real
  migration target (candidate surfaces `rbw-rrv/rvv/rnv`). enrollment-validation
  is pure-utility (BUV type checks via public `buv_vet`) — leave it, do not
  invent a test-only tabtarget for it. Kept as a single study-gated pace;
  decompose only if the crux below forces invented test-API.

- **Pivotal unknown (settle first at mount).** Whether a validate tabtarget can
  be pointed at a *staged malformed regime* decides whether regime-validation
  negatives are tabtarget-coverable (mechanical) or need a declared
  `*_t_validate` entry point (a design sub-task).

- **Reliability spine.** A pre-migration verdict baseline (per-case Pass/Fail
  across the fast suite) is the safety net — the post-migration verdict set must
  match exactly, and migrated negatives must fail for the *same reason*, not
  merely fail.

## Done looks like

Theurge owns no colophons; the four credential checks are operator tabtargets.
`rbtdrf_fast.rs`'s regime-validation family exercises tabtargets (or declared
test entry points), with no hardcoded module paths or `z`-private reach. Fast,
complete, and gauntlet suites pass with verdicts matching the pre-migration
baseline.

## References

- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrf_fast.rs` — migration surface
- `Tools/rbk/rbgv_AccessProbe.sh` — access-probe implementation (unchanged)
- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/rbte_cli.sh`, `rbte_engine.sh` — theurge colophon ownership to strip
- `Tools/rbk/rbz_zipper.sh`, `rbw_workbench.sh` — operator dispatch enrollment
- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrm_manifest.rs` — fixture-to-colophon dependency tables
- `rbtdri_invocation.rs` header — "theurge invokes bottle operations exclusively through tabtargets"

# jjezs_pace ₢BJAAE

## Character

Architecture move requiring judgment — one load-bearing risk (single-process fixture isolation) gates an otherwise mechanical collapse.

## Goal

Make theurge the sole owner of suite→fixture composition. Suite names survive only as tabtarget imprints; no suite name or fixture list appears anywhere in bash.

## Locked constraints

- Supersedes the "suite is bash composition" decision (₢BBAAh, ₣BB) — a release-qual conformance call, not a principled ownership stance.
- Suite membership is expressed in Rust by reference to the existing `RBTDRC_FIXTURES` statics, so a bad member fails at compile time — not a name-string list mirrored across the boundary.
- The dirty-tree guard and per-run root allocation move to once-per-suite; today they run once per fixture.

## Shape

- Rust gains a suite registry and the binary a suite mode (suite name in, its fixtures run); the single-fixture and `single` modes stay. Bash's suite arrays, the suite-resolve, and the per-fixture loop collapse to an imprint pass-through; the suite-rationale comments (gauntlet state-walk, skirmish/dogfight operator preconditions) migrate to Rust doc on the registry.
- Load-bearing risk: the per-fixture loop becomes one process instead of N. Confirm crucible quench runs on the case-failure path, and settle quench-on-panic, before relying on the in-process loop.

## Sources

- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/rbte_engine.sh` — suite arrays, resolve, `rbte_suite` loop
- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/main.rs` — binary modes, dirty-tree guard, root allocation
- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs` — `RBTDRC_FIXTURES` fixture statics
- the `rbw-ts.TestSuite.*` suite tabtargets — imprint channel

## What done looks like

Suite composition lives only in theurge; grep finds no suite names or fixture lists in bash; suite tabtargets pass only their imprint; a typo'd suite member fails compilation; all suites run green.

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# RBTE Engine - Theurge test engine implementation module
20	#
21	# BCG module providing kindle/sentinel/public functions for theurge
22	# Rust build, test, and orchestration commands.
23	
24	set -euo pipefail
25	
26	# Multiple inclusion guard
27	test -z "${ZRBTE_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
28	ZRBTE_SOURCED=1
29	
30	######################################################################
31	# Kindle
32	
33	zrbte_kindle() {
34	  test -z "${ZRBTE_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "rbte already kindled"
35	
36	  local z_dir="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
37	
38	  readonly RBTE_MANIFEST="${z_dir}/Cargo.toml"
39	  readonly ZRBTE_BINARY="${z_dir}/target/debug/rbtd"
40	
41	  # Suite-to-fixture mappings
42	  ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST=(
43	    "enrollment-validation"
44	    "regime-validation"
45	    "regime-smoke"
46	    "handbook-render"
47	    "dockerfile-hygiene"
48	  )
49	  ZRBTE_SUITE_SERVICE=("${ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST[@]}" "access-probe" "hallmark-lifecycle" "batch-vouch")
50	  ZRBTE_SUITE_CRUCIBLE=("${ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST[@]}" "tadmor" "srjcl" "pluml")
51	  ZRBTE_SUITE_COMPLETE=("${ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST[@]}" "access-probe" "hallmark-lifecycle" "batch-vouch" "tadmor" "srjcl" "pluml")
52	  # Gauntlet — release-qualification ladder, walks marshal-zero state through
53	  # canonical-credentialed state to crucible verification. Pristine-lifecycle
54	  # case 1 is the entry-contract gate; preceding enrollment-validation runs
55	  # state-indifferent and is harmless on broken state. Fail-fast across
56	  # fixtures is provided by the for-loop's set -e.
57	  ZRBTE_SUITE_GAUNTLET=(
58	    "enrollment-validation"
59	    "pristine-lifecycle"
60	    "canonical-establish"
61	    "onboarding-sequence"
62	    "regime-validation"
63	    "regime-smoke"
64	    "dockerfile-hygiene"
65	    "hallmark-lifecycle"
66	    "tadmor"
67	    "moriah"
68	    "srjcl"
69	    "pluml"
70	  )
71	  # Skirmish — the "mini gauntlet": the depot->build->crucible chain WITHOUT
72	  # project-ID churn. canonical-invest reuses a standing operator-levied depot
73	  # (no levy, no unmake) where the gauntlet's pristine-lifecycle/canonical
74	  # -establish each levy a fresh project; pristine-lifecycle is dropped
75	  # entirely. onboarding-sequence then builds the crucible images (local kludge
76	  # + cloud ordain into the standing depot) and the four crucibles charge+run.
77	  # OPERATOR PRECONDITION: a canonical depot already levied — install canonical
78	  # prefixes and run rbw-dL by hand before this suite. Spends cloud build/GAR
79	  # but creates no GCP project per run.
80	  ZRBTE_SUITE_SKIRMISH=(
81	    "enrollment-validation"
82	    "canonical-invest"
83	    "onboarding-sequence"
84	    "regime-validation"
85	    "regime-smoke"
86	    "dockerfile-hygiene"
87	    "tadmor"
88	    "moriah"
89	    "srjcl"
90	    "pluml"
91	  )
92	  # Dogfight — standing-depot cloud-build viability probe. Sibling to skirmish
93	  # in the operator-precondition family (reuses a hand-levied depot, no levy,
94	  # no unmake) but charges NO crucible: it proves only the cloud-build → summon
95	  # → run path yields a runnable artifact. canonical-invest leads exactly as it
96	  # does in skirmish — re-mantling the governor and divest/re-investing
97	  # retriever + director so the dogfight fixture finds fresh credentials; the
98	  # dogfight fixture itself stays crucible-free. OPERATOR PRECONDITION: a
99	  # canonical depot already levied (the standing-depot setup skirmish assumes).
100	  ZRBTE_SUITE_DOGFIGHT=(
101	    "canonical-invest"
102	    "dogfight"
103	  )
104	  # Tadmor self-contained — fully local, no GCP/depot/project. Two fixtures in
105	  # sequence: kludge-tadmor builds BOTH vessels (sentry + bottle) locally and
106	  # commits each hallmark (the fixture owns the notch — same precedent as
107	  # onboarding's rbtdro_kludge_nameplate); then the tadmor crucible fixture
108	  # charges against the now-clean nameplate, runs the security cases, quenches.
109	  # The build is a separate fixture (nameplate passed explicitly) rather than a
110	  # self-charging tadmor fixture, because the crucible security cases resolve
111	  # their nameplate from the fixture name and would collide on "tadmor".
112	  ZRBTE_SUITE_TADMOR=(
113	    "kludge-tadmor"
114	    "tadmor"
115	  )
116	
117	  readonly ZRBTE_KINDLED=1
118	}
119	
120	######################################################################
121	# Sentinel
122	
123	zrbte_sentinel() {
124	  test "${ZRBTE_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbte not kindled - call zrbte_kindle first"
125	}
126	
127	######################################################################
128	# Internal helpers
129	
130	# zrbte_codegen() - Refresh all zipper-derived artifacts before compiling.
131	# Write-on-change, so unchanged files keep their mtime and cargo skips rebuilds.
132	# The build is the sole producer — there is no standalone generate command.
133	zrbte_codegen() {
134	  zrbte_sentinel
135	
136	  rbz_generate_context "${BURD_TABTARGET_DIR}" "${RBCC_tabtarget_context_file}" \
137	    || buc_die "Failed to generate tabtarget context"
138	  rbz_generate_consts "${RBCC_rbtdgc_consts_file}" \
139	    || buc_die "Failed to generate colophon consts"
140	}
141	
142	zrbte_build_binary() {
143	  zrbte_sentinel
144	
145	  zrbte_codegen
146	  buc_step "Building theurge"
147	  cargo build --manifest-path "${RBTE_MANIFEST}" || buc_die "cargo build failed"
148	  test -x "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" || buc_die "Theurge binary not found: ${ZRBTE_BINARY}"
149	}
150	
151	zrbte_resolve_suite() {
152	  local z_suite="$1"
153	  case "${z_suite}" in
154	    fast)     echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST[*]}" ;;
155	    service)  echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_SERVICE[*]}" ;;
156	    crucible) echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_CRUCIBLE[*]}" ;;
157	    complete) echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_COMPLETE[*]}" ;;
158	    gauntlet) echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_GAUNTLET[*]}" ;;
159	    skirmish) echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_SKIRMISH[*]}" ;;
160	    dogfight) echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_DOGFIGHT[*]}" ;;
161	    tadmor)   echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_TADMOR[*]}" ;;
162	    *)        buc_die "Unknown suite: ${z_suite} (expected fast|service|crucible|complete|gauntlet|skirmish|dogfight|tadmor)" ;;
163	  esac
164	}
165	
166	######################################################################
167	# Public functions
168	
169	rbte_build() {
170	  zrbte_sentinel
171	
172	  zrbte_codegen
173	  buc_step "Building theurge"
174	  buc_log_args "Manifest: ${RBTE_MANIFEST}"
175	  cargo build --manifest-path "${RBTE_MANIFEST}" "$@" || buc_die "cargo build failed"
176	  buc_success "Theurge built"
177	}
178	
179	rbte_test() {
180	  zrbte_sentinel
181	
182	  zrbte_codegen
183	  buc_step "Testing theurge"
184	  buc_log_args "Manifest: ${RBTE_MANIFEST}"
185	  cargo test --manifest-path "${RBTE_MANIFEST}" "$@" || buc_die "cargo test failed"
186	  buc_success "All theurge tests passed"
187	}
188	
189	rbte_run() {
190	  zrbte_sentinel
191	
192	  local z_fixture="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
193	  test -n "${z_fixture}" || buc_die "No fixture — pass one as the folio (e.g. rbw-tf.FixtureRun.sh tadmor)"
194	
195	  zrbte_build_binary
196	
197	  buc_step "Running theurge fixture '${z_fixture}'"
198	  "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" "${ZRBZ_COLOPHON_MANIFEST}" "${z_fixture}"
199	}
200	
201	rbte_suite() {
202	  zrbte_sentinel
203	
204	  local z_suite="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
205	  test -n "${z_suite}" || buc_die "No suite imprint — use tabtarget with imprint (e.g. rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh)"
206	
207	  local z_fixture_list
208	  z_fixture_list="$(zrbte_resolve_suite "${z_suite}")"
209	
210	  zrbte_build_binary
211	
212	  local z_count=0
213	  local z_fixture
214	  for z_fixture in ${z_fixture_list}; do
215	    buc_step "Running theurge fixture '${z_fixture}'"
216	    "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" "${ZRBZ_COLOPHON_MANIFEST}" "${z_fixture}"
217	    z_count=$((z_count + 1))
218	  done
219	
220	  buc_success "Suite '${z_suite}' complete (${z_count} fixtures)"
221	}
222	
223	rbte_single() {
224	  zrbte_sentinel
225	
226	  zrbte_build_binary
227	
228	  local z_fixture="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
229	  local z_case="${1:-}"
230	  "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" single "${ZRBZ_COLOPHON_MANIFEST}" ${z_fixture:+"${z_fixture}"} ${z_case:+"${z_case}"}
231	}
232	
233	# eof
234

#

#

# Rust build, test, and orchestration commands.

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion guard
test -z "${ZRBTE_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
ZRBTE_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Kindle

zrbte_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBTE_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "rbte already kindled"

  local z_dir="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"

  readonly RBTE_MANIFEST="${z_dir}/Cargo.toml"
  readonly ZRBTE_BINARY="${z_dir}/target/debug/rbtd"

  # Suite-to-fixture mappings
  ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST=(
    "enrollment-validation"
    "regime-validation"
    "regime-smoke"
    "handbook-render"
    "dockerfile-hygiene"
  )
  ZRBTE_SUITE_SERVICE=("${ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST[@]}" "access-probe" "hallmark-lifecycle" "batch-vouch")
  ZRBTE_SUITE_CRUCIBLE=("${ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST[@]}" "tadmor" "srjcl" "pluml")
  ZRBTE_SUITE_COMPLETE=("${ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST[@]}" "access-probe" "hallmark-lifecycle" "batch-vouch" "tadmor" "srjcl" "pluml")
  # Gauntlet — release-qualification ladder, walks marshal-zero state through
  # canonical-credentialed state to crucible verification. Pristine-lifecycle
  # case 1 is the entry-contract gate; preceding enrollment-validation runs
  # state-indifferent and is harmless on broken state. Fail-fast across
  # fixtures is provided by the for-loop's set -e.
  ZRBTE_SUITE_GAUNTLET=(
    "enrollment-validation"
    "pristine-lifecycle"
    "canonical-establish"
    "onboarding-sequence"
    "regime-validation"
    "regime-smoke"
    "dockerfile-hygiene"
    "hallmark-lifecycle"
    "tadmor"
    "moriah"
    "srjcl"
    "pluml"
  )
  # Skirmish — the "mini gauntlet": the depot->build->crucible chain WITHOUT
  # project-ID churn. canonical-invest reuses a standing operator-levied depot
  # (no levy, no unmake) where the gauntlet's pristine-lifecycle/canonical
  # -establish each levy a fresh project; pristine-lifecycle is dropped
  # entirely. onboarding-sequence then builds the crucible images (local kludge
  # + cloud ordain into the standing depot) and the four crucibles charge+run.
  # OPERATOR PRECONDITION: a canonical depot already levied — install canonical
  # prefixes and run rbw-dL by hand before this suite. Spends cloud build/GAR
  # but creates no GCP project per run.
  ZRBTE_SUITE_SKIRMISH=(
    "enrollment-validation"
    "canonical-invest"
    "onboarding-sequence"
    "regime-validation"
    "regime-smoke"
    "dockerfile-hygiene"
    "tadmor"
    "moriah"
    "srjcl"
    "pluml"
  )
  # Dogfight — standing-depot cloud-build viability probe. Sibling to skirmish
  # in the operator-precondition family (reuses a hand-levied depot, no levy,
  # no unmake) but charges NO crucible: it proves only the cloud-build → summon
  # → run path yields a runnable artifact. canonical-invest leads exactly as it
  # does in skirmish — re-mantling the governor and divest/re-investing
  # retriever + director so the dogfight fixture finds fresh credentials; the
  # dogfight fixture itself stays crucible-free. OPERATOR PRECONDITION: a
  # canonical depot already levied (the standing-depot setup skirmish assumes).
  ZRBTE_SUITE_DOGFIGHT=(
    "canonical-invest"
    "dogfight"
  )
  # Tadmor self-contained — fully local, no GCP/depot/project. Two fixtures in
  # sequence: kludge-tadmor builds BOTH vessels (sentry + bottle) locally and
  # commits each hallmark (the fixture owns the notch — same precedent as
  # onboarding's rbtdro_kludge_nameplate); then the tadmor crucible fixture
  # charges against the now-clean nameplate, runs the security cases, quenches.
  # The build is a separate fixture (nameplate passed explicitly) rather than a
  # self-charging tadmor fixture, because the crucible security cases resolve
  # their nameplate from the fixture name and would collide on "tadmor".
  ZRBTE_SUITE_TADMOR=(
    "kludge-tadmor"
    "tadmor"
  )

  readonly ZRBTE_KINDLED=1
}

######################################################################
# Sentinel

zrbte_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBTE_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbte not kindled - call zrbte_kindle first"
}

######################################################################
# Internal helpers

# zrbte_codegen() - Refresh all zipper-derived artifacts before compiling.
# Write-on-change, so unchanged files keep their mtime and cargo skips rebuilds.
# The build is the sole producer — there is no standalone generate command.
zrbte_codegen() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  rbz_generate_context "${BURD_TABTARGET_DIR}" "${RBCC_tabtarget_context_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to generate tabtarget context"
  rbz_generate_consts "${RBCC_rbtdgc_consts_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to generate colophon consts"
}

zrbte_build_binary() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  zrbte_codegen
  buc_step "Building theurge"
  cargo build --manifest-path "${RBTE_MANIFEST}" || buc_die "cargo build failed"
  test -x "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" || buc_die "Theurge binary not found: ${ZRBTE_BINARY}"
}

zrbte_resolve_suite() {
  local z_suite="$1"
  case "${z_suite}" in
    fast)     echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST[*]}" ;;
    service)  echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_SERVICE[*]}" ;;
    crucible) echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_CRUCIBLE[*]}" ;;
    complete) echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_COMPLETE[*]}" ;;
    gauntlet) echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_GAUNTLET[*]}" ;;
    skirmish) echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_SKIRMISH[*]}" ;;
    dogfight) echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_DOGFIGHT[*]}" ;;
    tadmor)   echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_TADMOR[*]}" ;;
    *)        buc_die "Unknown suite: ${z_suite} (expected fast|service|crucible|complete|gauntlet|skirmish|dogfight|tadmor)" ;;
  esac
}

######################################################################
# Public functions

rbte_build() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  zrbte_codegen
  buc_step "Building theurge"
  buc_log_args "Manifest: ${RBTE_MANIFEST}"
  cargo build --manifest-path "${RBTE_MANIFEST}" "$@" || buc_die "cargo build failed"
  buc_success "Theurge built"
}

rbte_test() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  zrbte_codegen
  buc_step "Testing theurge"
  buc_log_args "Manifest: ${RBTE_MANIFEST}"
  cargo test --manifest-path "${RBTE_MANIFEST}" "$@" || buc_die "cargo test failed"
  buc_success "All theurge tests passed"
}

rbte_run() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  local z_fixture="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
  test -n "${z_fixture}" || buc_die "No fixture — pass one as the folio (e.g. rbw-tf.FixtureRun.sh tadmor)"

  zrbte_build_binary

  buc_step "Running theurge fixture '${z_fixture}'"
  "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" "${ZRBZ_COLOPHON_MANIFEST}" "${z_fixture}"
}

rbte_suite() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  local z_suite="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
  test -n "${z_suite}" || buc_die "No suite imprint — use tabtarget with imprint (e.g. rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh)"

  local z_fixture_list
  z_fixture_list="$(zrbte_resolve_suite "${z_suite}")"

  zrbte_build_binary

  local z_count=0
  local z_fixture
  for z_fixture in ${z_fixture_list}; do
    buc_step "Running theurge fixture '${z_fixture}'"
    "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" "${ZRBZ_COLOPHON_MANIFEST}" "${z_fixture}"
    z_count=$((z_count + 1))
  done

  buc_success "Suite '${z_suite}' complete (${z_count} fixtures)"
}

rbte_single() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  zrbte_build_binary

  local z_fixture="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
  local z_case="${1:-}"
  "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" single "${ZRBZ_COLOPHON_MANIFEST}" ${z_fixture:+"${z_fixture}"} ${z_case:+"${z_case}"}
}

# eof

/// single-case tabtarget emits on missing/unknown fixture arg. Declaration

pub static RBTDRC_FIXTURES: &[&'static rbtdre_Fixture] = &[
    &RBTDRC_FIXTURE_TADMOR,
    &RBTDRC_FIXTURE_MORIAH,
    &RBTDRC_FIXTURE_SRJCL,
    &RBTDRC_FIXTURE_PLUML,
    &RBTDRC_FIXTURE_HALLMARK_LIFECYCLE,
    &RBTDRC_FIXTURE_BATCH_VOUCH,
    &RBTDRC_FIXTURE_ACCESS_PROBE,
    &crate::rbtdrf_fast::RBTDRF_FIXTURE_ENROLLMENT_VALIDATION,
    &crate::rbtdrf_fast::RBTDRF_FIXTURE_REGIME_VALIDATION,
    &crate::rbtdrf_fast::RBTDRF_FIXTURE_REGIME_SMOKE,
    &crate::rbtdrf_fast::RBTDRF_FIXTURE_DOCKERFILE_HYGIENE,
    &crate::rbtdrf_handbook::RBTDRF_FIXTURE_HANDBOOK_RENDER,
    &crate::rbtdrp_pristine::RBTDRP_FIXTURE_PRISTINE_LIFECYCLE,
    &crate::rbtdrk_canonical::RBTDRK_FIXTURE_CANONICAL_ESTABLISH,
    &crate::rbtdrk_canonical::RBTDRK_FIXTURE_CANONICAL_INVEST,
    &crate::rbtdro_onboarding::RBTDRO_FIXTURE_ONBOARDING_SEQUENCE,
    &crate::rbtdro_onboarding::RBTDRO_FIXTURE_KLUDGE_TADMOR,
    &crate::rbtdrd_dogfight::RBTDRD_FIXTURE_DOGFIGHT,
    &crate::rbtdrl_calibrant::RBTDRL_FIXTURE_VERDICTS,
    &crate::rbtdrl_calibrant::RBTDRL_FIXTURE_FAIL_FAST,
    &crate::rbtdrl_calibrant::RBTDRL_FIXTURE_PROGRESSING,
    &crate::rbtdrl_calibrant::RBTDRL_FIXTURE_SENTINEL,
];

/// Resolve a fixture name to its registered Fixture definition. Returns None
/// for unregistered names; callers decide whether that is fatal.
pub fn rbtdrc_lookup_fixture(fixture: &str) -> Option<&'static rbtdre_Fixture> {
    RBTDRC_FIXTURES.iter().find(|f| f.name == fixture).copied()
}

content.text:
I'm oriented. Here's the mount.

---

## ₢BJAAE — theurge-owns-suite-composition

**Goal in one sentence:** Make theurge (the Rust binary) the sole owner of which fixtures compose each suite — so suite names survive only as tabtarget imprints, and no suite name or fixture list appears anywhere in bash.

**Heat context** — ₣BJ *rbk-14-mvp-testing-simplification* (racing, 3/4 done). The heat's shape: theurge should be a clean *consumer* of operator tabtargets, never an owner of commands or composition. Recent paces moved access-probes to operator tabtargets and migrated regime-validation onto a public probate contract. This last pace closes the composition-ownership gap.

**The docket** (shown in full above) — Character: *architecture move requiring judgment; one load-bearing risk gates an otherwise mechanical collapse.*

### Name assessment
`theurge-owns-suite-composition` fits the docket exactly. No rename.

### What I found surveying the sources

The collapse is real and clean:
- `rbte_engine.sh:42-164` holds 8 suite arrays, the suite-rationale comments (gauntlet state-walk, skirmish/dogfight operator preconditions, tadmor self-contained), `zrbte_resolve_suite`, and the per-fixture `rbte_suite` loop that spawns **one `rbtd` process per fixture** (`main.rs:101 rbtdb_run_suite`).
- `main.rs` already cleanly separates suite-runner vs `single` mode; adding a third `suite` mode is natural.
- **The load-bearing risk resolves favorably on the case-failure path:** `rbtdre_run_fixture` (`rbtdre_engine.rs:289`) is already finally-shaped — teardown (quench) runs unconditionally after cases regardless of verdict. So a *Fail verdict* already quenches. The genuinely open question is **quench-on-panic**: today a panicking case kills the whole process and skips teardown (charged crucible leaks); in an in-process N-fixture loop that same panic would also abort fixtures `i+1..N`. That's the one judgment call.

### Proposed approach
- **Rust suite registry**: a `RBTDRC_SUITES` static mapping each suite name → `&[&'static rbtdre_Fixture]` *by reference to the existing fixture statics* (bad member = compile error). The 8 rationale comment-blocks migrate to doc-comments on the registry entries.
- **Binary `suite` mode** in `main.rs`: dirty-tree guard + root allocation run **once per suite** (not per fixture); then loop fixtures, doing `set_context`/`run_fixture`/`take_context` per fixture, accumulating into one summary, fail-fast across fixtures to match today's `set -e`.
- **Settle quench-on-panic** before relying on the loop — wrap each fixture run in `catch_unwind` so a panicking case still triggers its teardown and the loop fails cleanly rather than leaking a charged crucible. This is the load-bearing decision to confirm with you.
- **Bash collapse**: `rbte_suite` becomes an imprint pass-through (`rbtd suite <manifest> <imprint>`); delete the suite arrays and `zrbte_resolve_suite`.

### Execution strategy
**Sequential, opus** — this is the architectural pace of the heat, not mechanical: the suite-mode loop, context lifecycle per-fixture, and the panic-isolation decision all need judgment, and they're coupled across `main.rs` + the new registry + `rbte_engine.sh`. Not parallelizable.

One open question for you before I start:

questions.question: The in-process suite loop changes panic behavior: today each fixture is its own process, so a panicking case can't leak state into sibling fixtures. In one process, how should a panicking case be handled?

options.label: catch_unwind + quench, fail-fast
options.description: Wrap each fixture in catch_unwind so a panicking case still runs its teardown (quench), record it as a fixture failure, then stop the suite (matching today's set -e fail-fast). Safest against charged-crucible leaks.

options.label: catch_unwind + quench, continue
options.description: Same isolation, but continue running remaining fixtures after a panic and report all failures at the end. Diverges from today's fail-fast.

options.label: Let panic abort process
options.description: Don't catch — a panic aborts the whole suite process as it does today (one fixture's crucible may leak charged). Simplest, but the in-process loop inherits the leak across the whole suite.

content.text:
Verified the crux. Here's the whole picture — and it makes your inclination the right call.

## How failure actually flows today

**Routine failure is `Verdict::Fail`, never a panic.** Every error path in the cases returns `rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(...)` (dozens of them above). The `.expect(...)` calls are harness invariants ("was context set?"), and the one `.unwrap()` is guarded by a prior emptiness check. A *panic* therefore isn't a test outcome — it's a harness bug (missing context, an unguarded unwrap slipping in later). `rbtdre_run_cases` does **not** `catch_unwind` today.

So there are two distinct paths, and only one is common:

| Path | Frequency | Quench today | Quench after this pace |
|------|-----------|--------------|------------------------|
| Case returns `Fail` | the 99% case | ✅ runs — teardown is finally-shaped (`rbtdre_run_fixture:308`, unconditional after cases) | ✅ unchanged |
| Case **panics** | harness bug only | ❌ skipped — panic unwinds *past* the teardown line; crucible leaks charged; bash `set -e` stops the suite | depends on this decision |

## The insight you were missing

The N-process model **does not actually protect against the panic leak.** When a fixture panics today, its quench is skipped and its crucible — containers, iptables rules, the enclave network — is left running. Process exit reclaims *process* memory and FDs, but the crucible is *external* state that survives the dead process. So today, a panic already leaks exactly one charged crucible and halts the suite.

An in-process loop with **"let panic abort"** produces the *identical* outcome: that one fixture's crucible leaks, the suite halts, fixtures after it don't run. Fixtures *before* it already charged-and-quenched cleanly in their own iterations (success-path teardown runs). **It's behavior-preserving — not a regression.**

The only thing `catch_unwind` would buy is *fixing* a latent pre-existing bug (quench the panicking fixture before stopping). That's a strict improvement over today, but it's orthogonal to this pace's goal (composition ownership) and scope-additive.

## My recommendation

**Go with your inclination: let the panic abort.** Rationale:
- It's behavior-identical to today, so this pace introduces zero new risk on the panic path.
- The common path (Fail verdict → quench) already works and stays working.
- `catch_unwind` carries its own complexity (`AssertUnwindSafe` around the thread-local context, deciding resume-vs-abort) that doesn't belong in an ownership-collapse pace.
- The leak-on-panic is real but pre-existing and rare; if you want it fixed, it's a clean standalone itch ("theurge skips quench when a case panics") rather than freight on this one.

One thing I'd add either way: a **one-line doc comment** on the suite loop noting that a panicking case aborts the suite with that fixture's crucible left charged — so the behavior is documented intent, not an accident a future reader has to rediscover.

Want me to proceed on that basis — abort-on-panic, documented — and optionally drop the leak-fix as an itch?

content.text:
Two good questions. Let me take the process one first, because I think there's a direction mix-up worth clearing.

## Does this drive theurge to be multiprocess?

**No — the exact opposite.** Today theurge *is* multiprocess for suites; this change makes it single-process.

Look at the current loop (`rbte_engine.sh:201-221`):

```bash
for z_fixture in ${z_fixture_list}; do
  "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" "${ZRBZ_COLOPHON_MANIFEST}" "${z_fixture}"   # ← one OS process per fixture
done
```

One `rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh` invocation spawns **N separate `rbtd` processes** — one per fixture, sequentially (5 for `fast`, 12 for `gauntlet`). Each process independently does: verify → tree-clean guard → allocate roots → charge → cases → quench → exit.

After this pace, bash calls the binary **once**:

```bash
"${ZRBTE_BINARY}" suite "${ZRBZ_COLOPHON_MANIFEST}" "${z_suite}"   # ← single process, loops fixtures internally
```

So the count goes **N → 1**. The docket's phrase "the per-fixture loop becomes one process instead of N" is describing this collapse. That's *why* it's the load-bearing risk: things the OS used to do for free at each process boundary (fresh address space, guaranteed teardown-by-death) now have to be handled in-process across fixtures — the per-fixture context set/take, the once-vs-N-times root allocation, and the panic question. Nothing here makes theurge *more* concurrent; suites stay strictly sequential either way (and CLAUDE.md forbids parallel fixtures regardless).

## "Drop the leak-fix as an itch" — what I meant

Sloppy phrasing on my part; "drop" was colloquial, not `jjx_drop`. Let me restate plainly.

There are **two separate things** in play:

1. **This pace's job** — move suite composition into Rust (the ownership collapse). That's the goal.
2. **A pre-existing latent bug** — when a case *panics* (not a normal `Fail`, but a genuine Rust panic), `rbtdre_run_fixture` skips the teardown line, so that fixture's crucible is left charged. This is true *today*, in the multiprocess model, and "let panic abort" keeps it true.

"Drop it as an itch" meant: **write bug #2 down as future work** (an *itch* — JJ's term for captured-but-not-now work, lives in `jji_itch.md`) so it isn't forgotten, rather than fixing it *inside* this pace. The alternative — fixing it here via `catch_unwind` — would be bolting an orthogonal reliability fix onto an ownership-refactor pace, which muddies both.

So three honest options for bug #2, independent of finishing the pace:
- **Leave it, write nothing** — it's rare and pre-existing; accept it silently.
- **Leave it, capture an itch** — note it so a future pace can fix it deliberately. (This is what I was suggesting.)
- **Fix it now** — add `catch_unwind` in this pace anyway.

My read is option 2: your "let panic abort" stands for *this* pace (zero regression, clean scope), and the panic-leak gets a one-line itch so it's not lost. But that's a call about how much you want riding on this pace — not a technical blocker.

Does that clear up both? And do you want the panic-leak captured as an itch, or left entirely alone?

245	/// Verify that all required colophons for a fixture appear in the zipper manifest string.
246	pub fn rbtdrm_verify(manifest: &str, fixture: &str) -> Result<(), String> {
247	    let required = match rbtdrm_required_colophons(fixture) {
248	        Some(r) => r,
249	        None => {
250	            return Err(format!(
251	                "rbtd: unknown fixture '{}' — not registered in manifest",
252	                fixture
253	            ));
254	        }
255	    };
256	    for colophon in required {
257	        let found = manifest.split_whitespace().any(|token| token == *colophon);
258	        if !found {
259	            return Err(format!(
260	                "rbtd: colophon '{}' not found in zipper manifest (fixture '{}')",
261	                colophon, fixture
262	            ));
263	        }
264	    }
265	    Ok(())
266	}

readonly RBTE_MANIFEST="${z_dir}/Cargo.toml"
  readonly ZRBTE_BINARY="${z_dir}/target/debug/rbtd"

  # Suite-to-fixture mappings
  ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST=(
    "enrollment-validation"
    "regime-validation"
    "regime-smoke"
    "handbook-render"
    "dockerfile-hygiene"
  )
  ZRBTE_SUITE_SERVICE=("${ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST[@]}" "access-probe" "hallmark-lifecycle" "batch-vouch")
  ZRBTE_SUITE_CRUCIBLE=("${ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST[@]}" "tadmor" "srjcl" "pluml")
  ZRBTE_SUITE_COMPLETE=("${ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST[@]}" "access-probe" "hallmark-lifecycle" "batch-vouch" "tadmor" "srjcl" "pluml")
  # Gauntlet — release-qualification ladder, walks marshal-zero state through
  # canonical-credentialed state to crucible verification. Pristine-lifecycle
  # case 1 is the entry-contract gate; preceding enrollment-validation runs
  # state-indifferent and is harmless on broken state. Fail-fast across
  # fixtures is provided by the for-loop's set -e.
  ZRBTE_SUITE_GAUNTLET=(
    "enrollment-validation"
    "pristine-lifecycle"
    "canonical-establish"
    "onboarding-sequence"
    "regime-validation"
    "regime-smoke"
    "dockerfile-hygiene"
    "hallmark-lifecycle"
    "tadmor"
    "moriah"
    "srjcl"
    "pluml"
  )
  # Skirmish — the "mini gauntlet": the depot->build->crucible chain WITHOUT
  # project-ID churn. canonical-invest reuses a standing operator-levied depot
  # (no levy, no unmake) where the gauntlet's pristine-lifecycle/canonical
  # -establish each levy a fresh project; pristine-lifecycle is dropped
  # entirely. onboarding-sequence then builds the crucible images (local kludge
  # + cloud ordain into the standing depot) and the four crucibles charge+run.
  # OPERATOR PRECONDITION: a canonical depot already levied — install canonical
  # prefixes and run rbw-dL by hand before this suite. Spends cloud build/GAR
  # but creates no GCP project per run.
  ZRBTE_SUITE_SKIRMISH=(
    "enrollment-validation"
    "canonical-invest"
    "onboarding-sequence"
    "regime-validation"
    "regime-smoke"
    "dockerfile-hygiene"
    "tadmor"
    "moriah"
    "srjcl"
    "pluml"
  )
  # Dogfight — standing-depot cloud-build viability probe. Sibling to skirmish
  # in the operator-precondition family (reuses a hand-levied depot, no levy,
  # no unmake) but charges NO crucible: it proves only the cloud-build → summon
  # → run path yields a runnable artifact. canonical-invest leads exactly as it
  # does in skirmish — re-mantling the governor and divest/re-investing
  # retriever + director so the dogfight fixture finds fresh credentials; the
  # dogfight fixture itself stays crucible-free. OPERATOR PRECONDITION: a
  # canonical depot already levied (the standing-depot setup skirmish assumes).
  ZRBTE_SUITE_DOGFIGHT=(
    "canonical-invest"
    "dogfight"
  )
  # Tadmor self-contained — fully local, no GCP/depot/project. Two fixtures in
  # sequence: kludge-tadmor builds BOTH vessels (sentry + bottle) locally and
  # commits each hallmark (the fixture owns the notch — same precedent as
  # onboarding's rbtdro_kludge_nameplate); then the tadmor crucible fixture
  # charges against the now-clean nameplate, runs the security cases, quenches.
  # The build is a separate fixture (nameplate passed explicitly) rather than a
  # self-charging tadmor fixture, because the crucible security cases resolve
  # their nameplate from the fixture name and would collide on "tadmor".
  ZRBTE_SUITE_TADMOR=(
    "kludge-tadmor"
    "tadmor"
  )

  readonly ZRBTE_KINDLED=1
}

# Rust build, test, and orchestration commands.

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion guard
test -z "${ZRBTE_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
ZRBTE_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Kindle

zrbte_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBTE_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "rbte already kindled"

  local z_dir="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"

  readonly RBTE_MANIFEST="${z_dir}/Cargo.toml"
  readonly ZRBTE_BINARY="${z_dir}/target/debug/rbtd"

  # Suite-to-fixture mappings
  ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST=(
    "enrollment-validation"
    "regime-validation"
    "regime-smoke"
    "handbook-render"
    "dockerfile-hygiene"
  )
  ZRBTE_SUITE_SERVICE=("${ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST[@]}" "access-probe" "hallmark-lifecycle" "batch-vouch")
  ZRBTE_SUITE_CRUCIBLE=("${ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST[@]}" "tadmor" "srjcl" "pluml")
  ZRBTE_SUITE_COMPLETE=("${ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST[@]}" "access-probe" "hallmark-lifecycle" "batch-vouch" "tadmor" "srjcl" "pluml")
  # Gauntlet — release-qualification ladder, walks marshal-zero state through
  # canonical-credentialed state to crucible verification. Pristine-lifecycle
  # case 1 is the entry-contract gate; preceding enrollment-validation runs
  # state-indifferent and is harmless on broken state. Fail-fast across
  # fixtures is provided by the for-loop's set -e.
  ZRBTE_SUITE_GAUNTLET=(
    "enrollment-validation"
    "pristine-lifecycle"
    "canonical-establish"
    "onboarding-sequence"
    "regime-validation"
    "regime-smoke"
    "dockerfile-hygiene"
    "hallmark-lifecycle"
    "tadmor"
    "moriah"
    "srjcl"
    "pluml"
  )
  # Skirmish — the "mini gauntlet": the depot->build->crucible chain WITHOUT
  # project-ID churn. canonical-invest reuses a standing operator-levied depot
  # (no levy, no unmake) where the gauntlet's pristine-lifecycle/canonical
  # -establish each levy a fresh project; pristine-lifecycle is dropped
  # entirely. onboarding-sequence then builds the crucible images (local kludge
  # + cloud ordain into the standing depot) and the four crucibles charge+run.
  # OPERATOR PRECONDITION: a canonical depot already levied — install canonical
  # prefixes and run rbw-dL by hand before this suite. Spends cloud build/GAR
  # but creates no GCP project per run.
  ZRBTE_SUITE_SKIRMISH=(
    "enrollment-validation"
    "canonical-invest"
    "onboarding-sequence"
    "regime-validation"
    "regime-smoke"
    "dockerfile-hygiene"
    "tadmor"
    "moriah"
    "srjcl"
    "pluml"
  )
  # Dogfight — standing-depot cloud-build viability probe. Sibling to skirmish
  # in the operator-precondition family (reuses a hand-levied depot, no levy,
  # no unmake) but charges NO crucible: it proves only the cloud-build → summon
  # → run path yields a runnable artifact. canonical-invest leads exactly as it
  # does in skirmish — re-mantling the governor and divest/re-investing
  # retriever + director so the dogfight fixture finds fresh credentials; the
  # dogfight fixture itself stays crucible-free. OPERATOR PRECONDITION: a
  # canonical depot already levied (the standing-depot setup skirmish assumes).
  ZRBTE_SUITE_DOGFIGHT=(
    "canonical-invest"
    "dogfight"
  )
  # Tadmor self-contained — fully local, no GCP/depot/project. Two fixtures in
  # sequence: kludge-tadmor builds BOTH vessels (sentry + bottle) locally and
  # commits each hallmark (the fixture owns the notch — same precedent as
  # onboarding's rbtdro_kludge_nameplate); then the tadmor crucible fixture
  # charges against the now-clean nameplate, runs the security cases, quenches.
  # The build is a separate fixture (nameplate passed explicitly) rather than a
  # self-charging tadmor fixture, because the crucible security cases resolve
  # their nameplate from the fixture name and would collide on "tadmor".
  ZRBTE_SUITE_TADMOR=(
    "kludge-tadmor"
    "tadmor"
  )

  readonly ZRBTE_KINDLED=1
}

######################################################################
# Sentinel

zrbte_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBTE_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbte not kindled - call zrbte_kindle first"
}

######################################################################
# Internal helpers

# zrbte_codegen() - Refresh all zipper-derived artifacts before compiling.
# Write-on-change, so unchanged files keep their mtime and cargo skips rebuilds.
# The build is the sole producer — there is no standalone generate command.
zrbte_codegen() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  rbz_generate_context "${BURD_TABTARGET_DIR}" "${RBCC_tabtarget_context_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to generate tabtarget context"
  rbz_generate_consts "${RBCC_rbtdgc_consts_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to generate colophon consts"
}

zrbte_build_binary() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  zrbte_codegen
  buc_step "Building theurge"
  cargo build --manifest-path "${RBTE_MANIFEST}" || buc_die "cargo build failed"
  test -x "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" || buc_die "Theurge binary not found: ${ZRBTE_BINARY}"
}

zrbte_resolve_suite() {
  local z_suite="$1"
  case "${z_suite}" in
    fast)     echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST[*]}" ;;
    service)  echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_SERVICE[*]}" ;;
    crucible) echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_CRUCIBLE[*]}" ;;
    complete) echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_COMPLETE[*]}" ;;
    gauntlet) echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_GAUNTLET[*]}" ;;
    skirmish) echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_SKIRMISH[*]}" ;;
    dogfight) echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_DOGFIGHT[*]}" ;;
    tadmor)   echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_TADMOR[*]}" ;;
    *)        buc_die "Unknown suite: ${z_suite} (expected fast|service|crucible|complete|gauntlet|skirmish|dogfight|tadmor)" ;;
  esac
}

######################################################################
# Public functions

rbte_build() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  zrbte_codegen
  buc_step "Building theurge"
  buc_log_args "Manifest: ${RBTE_MANIFEST}"
  cargo build --manifest-path "${RBTE_MANIFEST}" "$@" || buc_die "cargo build failed"
  buc_success "Theurge built"
}

rbte_test() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  zrbte_codegen
  buc_step "Testing theurge"
  buc_log_args "Manifest: ${RBTE_MANIFEST}"
  cargo test --manifest-path "${RBTE_MANIFEST}" "$@" || buc_die "cargo test failed"
  buc_success "All theurge tests passed"
}

rbte_run() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  local z_fixture="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
  test -n "${z_fixture}" || buc_die "No fixture — pass one as the folio (e.g. rbw-tf.FixtureRun.sh tadmor)"

  zrbte_build_binary

  buc_step "Running theurge fixture '${z_fixture}'"
  "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" "${ZRBZ_COLOPHON_MANIFEST}" "${z_fixture}"
}

rbte_suite() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  local z_suite="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
  test -n "${z_suite}" || buc_die "No suite imprint — use tabtarget with imprint (e.g. rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh)"

  local z_fixture_list
  z_fixture_list="$(zrbte_resolve_suite "${z_suite}")"

  zrbte_build_binary

  local z_count=0
  local z_fixture
  for z_fixture in ${z_fixture_list}; do
    buc_step "Running theurge fixture '${z_fixture}'"
    "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" "${ZRBZ_COLOPHON_MANIFEST}" "${z_fixture}"
    z_count=$((z_count + 1))
  done

  buc_success "Suite '${z_suite}' complete (${z_count} fixtures)"
}

rbte_single() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  zrbte_build_binary

  local z_fixture="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
  local z_case="${1:-}"
  "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" single "${ZRBZ_COLOPHON_MANIFEST}" ${z_fixture:+"${z_fixture}"} ${z_case:+"${z_case}"}
}

# eof

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# RBQ Qualify - Qualification orchestrator for tabtarget and colophon health

set -euo pipefail

ZRBQ_SCRIPT_DIR="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buq_qualify.sh"

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBQ_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbq multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBQ_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbq_*)

zrbq_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBQ_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbq already kindled"

  zbuz_sentinel
  zrbz_sentinel
  zrbcc_sentinel

  test -n "${BURC_TABTARGET_DIR:-}" || buc_die "BURC_TABTARGET_DIR not set"
  test -n "${BURC_TOOLS_DIR:-}"     || buc_die "BURC_TOOLS_DIR not set"

  readonly ZRBQ_TT_DIR="${BURC_TABTARGET_DIR}"
  readonly ZRBQ_PROJECT_ROOT="${BURC_TOOLS_DIR}/.."
  readonly ZRBQ_RBW_DIR="${RBCC_KIT_DIR}"
  readonly ZRBQ_RBW_LAUNCHER="${RBCC_moorings_dir}/${RBCC_launchers_subdir}/launcher.rbw_workbench.sh"

  readonly ZRBQ_KINDLED=1
}

zrbq_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBQ_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbq not kindled - call zrbq_kindle first"
}

######################################################################
# External Functions (rbq_*)

rbq_qualify_colophons() {
  zrbq_sentinel

  buc_step "Qualifying RBW colophon registrations"

  local z_fail_files=()
  local z_fail_reasons=()
  local z_checked=0

  local z_file=""
  for z_file in "${ZRBQ_TT_DIR}"/*.sh; do
    test -e "${z_file}" || continue

    local z_basename="${z_file##*/}"

    # Load file lines (load-then-iterate)
    local z_lines=()
    local z_line=""
    while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
      z_lines+=("${z_line}")
    done < "${z_file}"

    # Only check tabtargets using the RBW workbench launcher
    local z_is_rbw=0
    if (( ${#z_lines[@]} > 1 )); then
      case "${z_lines[1]}" in
        *"${ZRBQ_RBW_LAUNCHER}"*) z_is_rbw=1 ;;
      esac
    fi
    test "${z_is_rbw}" = "1" || continue

    z_checked=$((z_checked + 1))

    # Extract colophon from filename (everything before first delimiter)
    local z_colophon="${z_basename%%.*}"

    # Check colophon exists in z_buz_colophon_roll registry
    local z_found=0
    local z_i=0
    for z_i in "${!z_buz_colophon_roll[@]}"; do
      test "${z_buz_colophon_roll[$z_i]}" = "${z_colophon}" || continue
      z_found=1

      # Verify module file exists
      local z_module="${z_buz_module_roll[$z_i]}"
      test -f "${ZRBQ_RBW_DIR}/${z_module}" || {
        z_fail_files+=("${z_basename}")
        z_fail_reasons+=("module not found: ${z_module}")
      }
      break
    done

    test "${z_found}" = "1" || {
      z_fail_files+=("${z_basename}")
      z_fail_reasons+=("colophon '${z_colophon}' not registered in rbz_zipper")
    }
  done

  buc_log_args "Checked ${z_checked} RBW tabtargets"

  if (( ${#z_fail_files[@]} )); then
    local z_j=0
    for z_j in "${!z_fail_files[@]}"; do
      buc_warn "${z_fail_files[$z_j]}: ${z_fail_reasons[$z_j]}"
    done
    buc_die "Colophon qualification failed: ${#z_fail_files[@]} of ${z_checked} tabtargets"
  fi

  buc_log_args "All ${z_checked} RBW colophons registered"
}

rbq_qualify_context() {
  zrbq_sentinel

  buc_step "Qualifying generated context freshness"

  local -r z_committed="${RBCC_tabtarget_context_file}"
  local -r z_fresh="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbq_context_check.md"

  buz_emit_context "${ZRBQ_TT_DIR}" > "${z_fresh}" || buc_die "Failed to generate context for comparison"

  if ! test -f "${z_committed}"; then
    buc_die "Generated context file missing: ${z_committed} — run tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh"
  fi

  if ! diff -q "${z_fresh}" "${z_committed}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    buc_die "Generated context file is stale: ${z_committed} — run tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh"
  fi

  buc_log_args "Context file is fresh"
}

rbq_qualify_rust_consts() {
  zrbq_sentinel

  buc_step "Qualifying generated Rust consts freshness"

  local -r z_committed="${RBCC_rbtdgc_consts_file}"
  local -r z_fresh="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbq_rust_consts_check.rs"

  rbz_emit_consts > "${z_fresh}" || buc_die "Failed to generate Rust consts for comparison"

  if ! test -f "${z_committed}"; then
    buc_die "Generated Rust consts file missing: ${z_committed} — run tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh"
  fi

  if ! diff -q "${z_fresh}" "${z_committed}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    buc_die "Generated Rust consts file is stale: ${z_committed} — run tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh"
  fi

  buc_log_args "Rust consts file is fresh"
}

rbq_qualify_fast() {
  zrbq_sentinel

  buc_step "Running fast qualification"

  buq_tabtargets "${ZRBQ_TT_DIR}" "${ZRBQ_PROJECT_ROOT}" \
    "butctt.*.sh" \
    "buw-SI.*.sh" \
    "z-launcher.sh" \
    # End of exempt list
  rbq_qualify_colophons
  rbq_qualify_context
  rbq_qualify_rust_consts
  rbrn_preflight

  buc_step "Running shellcheck"
  buq_shellcheck "${BURC_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/busc_shellcheckrc" \
    "${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/buq_shellcheck_results.txt"

  buc_step "Fast qualification passed"
}

rbq_qualify_release() {
  zrbq_sentinel

  buc_step "Running release qualification"

  # Phase 1: Fast qualification (tabtargets, colophons, nameplate preflight, shellcheck)
  rbq_qualify_fast

  # Phase 2: Complete test suite (sequential)
  buc_step "Running complete test suite"
  "${ZRBQ_PROJECT_ROOT}/tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.complete.sh"

  buc_step "Release qualification passed"
}

content.text:
All findings are the standard `SC2154`/`SC2034`/`SC1091` patterns from the BCG kindle/zipper machinery (variables assigned via sourced modules) that the project's `busc_shellcheckrc` suppresses — and **every one is in a line I didn't touch** (the `rbte_engine.sh` hits are in `zrbte_codegen`; the `rbq_Qualify.sh` hits are all outside my comment edit). My edits introduced zero new findings; the qualify-fast shellcheck *step* is broken here only because shellcheck 0.9.0 doesn't accept the `--rcfile` flag the harness passes — a pre-existing environment issue.

## ₢BJAAE status — functionally complete

Checking against the docket's "what done looks like":

| Done-condition | Status |
|---|---|
| Suite composition lives only in theurge | ✅ `RBTDRC_SUITES` registry in `rbtdrc_crucible.rs` |
| grep finds no suite names / fixture lists in bash | ✅ confirmed none in `Tools/rbk/rbtd/*.sh` |
| Suite tabtargets pass only their imprint | ✅ `rbte_suite` → `rbtd suite <manifest> <imprint>` pass-through |
| A typo'd suite member fails compilation | ✅ members are `&'static` refs to fixture statics; build is the gate |
| All suites run green | ⚠️ **`fast` verified 112/112** end-to-end; the rest need deps |

Plus the load-bearing items: dirty-tree guard and root allocation now run **once per suite** (saw the guard fire once, `main.rs:228`); the per-fixture loop is one in-process loop, fail-fast across fixtures matching the old `set -e`; abort-on-panic is the documented, behavior-preserving choice we settled on.

Also done: deleted the 8 bash suite arrays + `zrbte_resolve_suite`; migrated the gauntlet/skirmish/dogfight/tadmor rationale to Rust doc on the registry; renamed the misnamed `rbtdb_run_suite` (a single-fixture runner) → `rbtdb_run_fixture` and gave the real suite runner that name. Committed as `5bd62114e`.

**The one gap:** "all suites run green" — I verified `fast` (no deps). The heavier suites need GCP credentials (`service`), container runtime (`crucible`), or operator-levied depots (`gauntlet`/`skirmish`/`dogfight`). Their *composition* is now compile-verified and they share the same suite-mode loop, but I haven't executed them.

How would you like to close the gap before wrapping?

The crucible suite validated the key behaviors — but tadmor's **charge** failed (exit 1), and the loop did exactly the right things around it:

- **5 fast fixtures: 112 passed** ✓
- **tadmor charge failed → quench still ran → "Crucible inactive confirmed"** ✓ — this is the finally-shaped teardown firing on the setup-failure path, the exact load-bearing behavior we cared about.
- **Fail-fast worked**: stopped at tadmor (6th), did not run srjcl/pluml ✓
- **The Err branch in my suite runner reported correctly**: `rbtd: fixture 'tadmor': setup failed: Charge failed` and counted 1 failed ✓

The question is whether the charge failure is *mine* or environmental. The charge hook is unchanged by this pace, and the `crucible` suite assumes tadmor's vessel images are already kludged (only the `tadmor` suite builds them first). Let me isolate: run tadmor through the **unchanged single-fixture path** (`rbtdb_run_fixture`) and read why charge failed.

content.text:
Confirmed: this is an **environmental precondition, not a regression**. The charge failed because there are **no tadmor vessel images built locally** (the `docker images` check returned nothing, and the charge's `CrucibleActive` probe — `BURX_TABTARGET=rbw-cic...`, `BURX_EXIT_STATUS=1` — found nothing charged). The `crucible` suite assumes the tadmor/srjcl/pluml images are already kludged; this environment hasn't built them. My suite-composition change is not involved — the charge hook is untouched, and the same charge would fail identically through the unchanged single-fixture path.

**What the crucible run *did* validate (the part that matters):**
- The per-fixture charge → (cases) → quench lifecycle fires correctly inside the in-process loop.
- **Quench-on-setup-failure works** — charge failed, yet teardown ran and "Crucible inactive confirmed." That's the finally-shaped teardown on the failure path, empirically confirmed.
- **Fail-fast across fixtures works** — stopped at tadmor, never ran srjcl/pluml.
- My Err branch reported and counted correctly.

**What it did *not* reach:** a *successful* multi-crucible run — charge tadmor, quench, then charge srjcl in the *same process* — which is the real in-process isolation question (shared roots, context reset between fixtures). The missing images blocked it.

The clean way to test that here, without GCP or pre-built images, is the **`tadmor` suite** (`kludge-tadmor` → `tadmor`): fully self-contained, builds the tadmor vessels locally, then charges/runs/quenches them — two fixtures in one process, exercising exactly the lifecycle-across-fixtures path. One caveat: the `kludge-tadmor` fixture **commits hallmark notches** into the nameplate (it owns that notch by design), so running it mutates the repo with additive commits.

questions.question: The crucible suite blocked on missing local images (environmental, not the change). To validate a successful multi-fixture charge/quench in one process without GCP, the self-contained `tadmor` suite is the right vehicle — but kludge-tadmor commits hallmark notches into the nameplate. How do you want to proceed?

options.label: Run the tadmor suite
options.description: Run tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.tadmor.sh — self-contained, builds tadmor vessels locally then charges/runs/quenches in one process. Validates the in-process lifecycle-across-fixtures path. Will add hallmark-notch commits to the repo.

options.label: Validation is sufficient
options.description: The fast suite (112/112), the quench-on-failure + fail-fast evidence from the crucible run, and compile-verified composition are enough. Don't run more; proceed toward wrap.

options.label: I'll validate crucible myself
options.description: You'll build images / run the heavier suites in your own environment. I stop here.

content.text:
Found the real cause — and it's neither my change nor missing images. The `Charge.tadmor` tabtarget runs a **qualification-gate preflight** (`rbq_qualify_fast`) through `rbw_workbench.sh`, and that gate fails on the **same shellcheck `--rcfile` incompatibility** I flagged earlier (`unrecognized option --rcfile`; shellcheck 0.9.0 here). The charge dies at `rbw_workbench.sh:68: Qualification gate failed` — before any container work.

So **every charge operation is blocked in this environment** by the pre-existing shellcheck tooling mismatch, entirely independent of this pace. That's why both crucible and tadmor charges failed identically.

This is actually a clean result for the pace: kludge-tadmor **passed in-process**, the context reset, and the second fixture's charge was correctly attempted with the right per-fixture context — it only died at the unrelated gate. The two-fixture in-process sequencing works; a *successful* charge is simply unreachable here.

Let me check what the suite committed (kludge-tadmor owns notches) and confirm the tree state:

input.command: echo "=== git status ==="; git status --short; echo "=== recent commits ==="; git log --oneline -6

Add rbtdre_Suite struct (engine) and the RBTDRC_SUITES registry + rbtdrc_lookup_suite (crucible), keyed by reference to the existing fixture statics so a mistyped/deleted member fails compilation; the gauntlet/skirmish/dogfight/tadmor rationale comments migrate from bash onto the registry entries. Binary gains a `suite` mode (rbtdb_run_suite): verifies each member's colophons up front, runs the dirty-tree guard and root allocation once per suite (was once per fixture under the bash loop), then loops fixtures with per-fixture context set/take, fail-fast across fixtures matching the old `set -e`, one aggregate summary. Documents that a panicking case aborts the suite with its crucible charged -- the same leak the per-process loop had; routine Fail verdicts quench normally via finally-shaped teardown. The misnamed single-fixture runner rbtdb_run_suite renamed to rbtdb_run_fixture. Bash collapses: ZRBTE_SUITE_* arrays and zrbte_resolve_suite deleted, rbte_suite is now an imprint pass-through to `rbtd suite`. rbq_Qualify comment repointed to the Rust registry. Build clean under deny(warnings), 117 theurge unit tests pass.

lastPrompt: Itches alas are not useful right now, but I haven't properly erased them from the context.  Proceed on that basis.

content.text:
Tree is clean. The tadmor suite added two **additive** hallmark-notch commits (`1cc64f36e` sentry, `966874838` bottle) — the locally-built tadmor vessels, exactly the notches `kludge-tadmor` owns by design. My pace commit `5bd62114e` sits intact beneath them. I've left those notch commits in place (additive, legitimate, not mine to discard).

## ₢BJAAE — final state

**Validated (as far as this environment allows):**
- ✅ `fast` suite **112/112** green end-to-end through the new `suite` mode, with the new aggregate summary line
- ✅ 117 theurge unit tests pass; build clean under `#![deny(warnings)]`
- ✅ Composition is **compile-verified** — every suite member is a `&'static` ref to a fixture static
- ✅ In-process loop behaviors all confirmed empirically:
  - **fail-fast** across fixtures (stopped at the failing fixture, skipped the rest)
  - **quench-on-failure** — teardown ran and confirmed inactive even when setup failed (the load-bearing concern, settled)
  - **per-fixture context reset** — `kludge-tadmor` ran in-process, context reset, the next fixture's charge was attempted with correct per-fixture context
- ✅ Dirty-tree guard + root allocation now fire **once per suite** (observed)
- ✅ Bash collapsed; grep finds no suite names or fixture lists in `Tools/rbk/rbtd/*.sh`

**Blocked by environment, not by this pace:**
- A *successful* charge is unreachable here. Every `Charge.*` tabtarget runs a `rbq_qualify_fast` preflight through `rbw_workbench.sh`, and that gate fails on a pre-existing **shellcheck 0.9.0 `--rcfile` incompatibility** (`unrecognized option --rcfile=Tools/buk/busc_shellcheckrc`). This blocks *all* charge operations regardless of my work — so `crucible`, `tadmor`, `service`, `gauntlet`, etc. can't go fully green in this environment. Worth knowing as a station-tooling gap (the installed shellcheck predates `--rcfile`, or the harness should use `.shellcheckrc` discovery), but it's orthogonal to this pace.

The pace's own "all suites run green" condition is satisfied to the limit of what's runnable here; the remaining suites are blocked by that tooling issue, not by the composition change.

I won't wrap without your say-so. **Ready to wrap ₢BJAAE?** — or would you like me to do anything further first (e.g., look into the shellcheck `--rcfile` gate, since it's currently blocking every charge on this station)?

message.content: look into the shellcheck --rcfile gate first

content.text: Confirmed: `--rcfile` was added in shellcheck **0.10.0**; this station has **0.9.0**. Let me read the full function and the rcfile to understand the fix surface:

173	  local z_rcfile="${2:-${BURD_BUK_DIR:+${BURD_BUK_DIR}/busc_shellcheckrc}}"
174	  local z_result_file="${3:-${BURD_TEMP_DIR:+${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/buq_shellcheck_results.txt}}"
175	  test -n "${z_tools_dir}"   || buc_die "buq_shellcheck: tools directory required"

file.content:
buq_shellcheck() {
  local z_tools_dir="${1:-${BURD_TOOLS_DIR:-}}"
  local z_rcfile="${2:-${BURD_BUK_DIR:+${BURD_BUK_DIR}/busc_shellcheckrc}}"
  local z_result_file="${3:-${BURD_TEMP_DIR:+${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/buq_shellcheck_results.txt}}"
  test -n "${z_tools_dir}"   || buc_die "buq_shellcheck: tools directory required"
  test -n "${z_rcfile}"      || buc_die "buq_shellcheck: rcfile path required"
  test -n "${z_result_file}" || buc_die "buq_shellcheck: result file path required"

  buc_step "Running shellcheck qualification"

  test -f "${z_rcfile}"    || buc_die "Shellcheck rcfile not found: ${z_rcfile}"
  test -d "${z_tools_dir}" || buc_die "Tools directory not found: ${z_tools_dir}"

  command -v shellcheck >/dev/null 2>&1 || buc_die "shellcheck not found — install from https://www.shellcheck.net"

  # Collect .sh files (load-then-iterate per BCG)
  local z_files=()
  local z_file=""
  while IFS= read -r z_file || test -n "${z_file}"; do
    z_files+=("${z_file}")
  done < <(find "${z_tools_dir}" -name '*.sh' -type f | sort)

  local -r z_file_count=${#z_files[@]}
  buc_log_args "Found ${z_file_count} shell files under ${z_tools_dir}"

  test "${z_file_count}" -gt 0 || buc_die "No .sh files found under ${z_tools_dir}"

  # Run shellcheck — capture output to temp file for forensics
  local z_status=0
  shellcheck --rcfile="${z_rcfile}" -S style -f gcc "${z_files[@]}" \
    > "${z_result_file}" 2>&1 \
    || z_status=$?

  if test "${z_status}" = "0"; then
    buc_step "Shellcheck qualification passed: ${z_file_count} files clean"
    return 0
  fi

  # Count and display findings
  local z_finding_count=0
  local z_discard
  while IFS= read -r z_discard || test -n "${z_discard:-}"; do
    z_finding_count=$((z_finding_count + 1))
  done < "${z_result_file}"
  buc_log_args "Shellcheck findings: ${z_finding_count} (see ${z_result_file})"

  local z_line=""
  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
    buc_warn "${z_line}"
  done < "${z_result_file}"

  buc_die "Shellcheck qualification failed: ${z_finding_count} findings across ${z_file_count} files"
}

# eof
